title: What Does Freedom Look Like to Me?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring what real freedom means in daily life, and what it would look like to live from that quality now
excerpt: A journal prompt set from the Day 80 theme of a 12-week money programme. The prompts open with a wide look at what freedom actually means, then narrow to the specific ways it is already present and how to live from it today rather than later.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 80 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to define what freedom actually means to you in specific, daily terms, and find evidence that it is already present.

content.text: A journal prompt set from the Day 80 theme of a 12-week money programme. Day 80: celebrate a daily life of freedom and joy. The prompts look at the specific shape of freedom that wealth already supports, and at the simple pleasure that is evidence of it today.
New to journal prompts as practice? Read
What does true freedom look like to me, in time, emotion, and choice?
Write the specific shape of it, not a general idea of it.
Where in my week do I already feel it?
Find at least one hour or moment that already carries the quality you named.
What would it look like to treat joy as productive and pleasure as progress?
Name a specific thing you keep treating as a reward rather than a standard.
How can I live from that energy today, not when I reach a certain goal?
Name one concrete change to how today is structured.
What simple pleasure today reminds me that joy and wealth are already the same?
Find the specific example. Write it out in one sentence.
Adapted from the Day 80 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
